headline: Trade Desk (TTD) Is Down 9.4% After Publicis Audit, Kokai Concerns And CEO Share Buying - Has The Bull Case Changed?
summary: In early March 2026, The Trade Desk faced turbulence as board member Gokul Rajaram resigned and French advertising group Publicis stopped recommending its platform after an audit found issues with fees, billing and the behavior of its AI tool Kokai. At the same time, a roughly US$148,000,000 insider share purchase by CEO Jeff Green and reports of early collaboration talks with OpenAI highlighted both management’s conviction and the company’s potential role in emerging AI-driven digital...

headline: A Look At Trade Desk (TTD) Valuation After Insider Buying And OpenAI Partnership Speculation
summary: A sharp shift in sentiment around Trade Desk (TTD) has followed CEO Jeff Green’s roughly US$148 million share purchase, rising institutional interest, and speculation about a possible OpenAI advertising tie up. See our latest analysis for Trade Desk. At a share price of US$25.07, Trade Desk is coming off a sharp reset in expectations, with a 90 day share price return of 32.55% decline and a 1 year total shareholder return of 53.22% loss. Investors are weighing the Publicis audit dispute and...

headline: Trade Desk Reels as Publicis Report Triggers a Two-Day, 13% Selloff
summary: Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD) shares are extending a painful two-day slide in Wednesday’s premarket, dropping another 5.42% to $23.65 after closing down 7.42% to $25.07 on Tuesday. The combined move brings the two-day loss to roughly 13%, and it’s entirely company-specific. While the S&P 500 fell just 0.1% and the communication services sector barely budged on ... headline: First AI Attacks, Now This: Should You Avoid The Trade Desk?
summary: The past year has not been kind to The Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD). Its stock has lost 75% of its value from its 52-week high. Despite management insisting AI would serve as a powerful tailwind — and rapidly deploying its own Kokai AI platform — the technology’s advances, deeply integrated into “walled gardens” at Google, Meta ... headline: Why The Trade Desk (TTD) Stock Is Nosediving
summary: Shares of digital advertising platform The Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD) fell 5.9% in the afternoon session after the stock continued to pull back as reports revealed that Publicis informed its clients to avoid the ad-tech platform following an audit report that found issues with its billing practices and fee transparency.

headline: The Trade Desk Stock Drops. 2 Downgrades Make a Bad Year Worse.
summary: The Trade Desk  stock was dropping Wednesday after two analysts downgraded the stock following an advertising company’s decision to stop recommending the digital ad platform to customers.  French ad firm  Publicis  told Barron’s that an independent auditor concluded that  The Trade Desk  did not pass an audit, and “as a result of the audit findings we will no longer be recommending The Trade Desk as a solution for our clients.”  “We look forward to working with Publicis to provide workable alternatives to this particular request, including information at an even more granular level than requested,” The Trade Desk added.
